title: podman images command errors after manifest-list build
body:
### Issue Description

After building [PodmanHello](https://github.com/containers/PodmanHello) with the `--manifest` option, the `podman images` command starts throwing an error.  I'm able to `podman run...` the image just fine.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Clone https://github.com/containers/PodmanHello.git
2. `cd PodmanHello`
3. `podman build --layers --force-rm --jobs=4 --platform=linux/amd64,linux/ppc64le,linux/s390x,linux/arm64 --manifest=hello ./`
4. `podman run -it --rm hello`
5. `podman images`

### Describe the results you received

The multi-arch parallel build works fine (I have qemu-static installed).  Running the image works fine.  Running `podman images` breaks horribly:

```
Error: fetching target platform image selected from image index: reading manifest for image instance "sha256:af880410d8507f37178d0e45d62123fe4adc85d85394569276cb3abcf1afcfd6": locating item named "manifest-sha256:af880410d8507f37178d0e45d62123fe4adc85d85394569276cb3abcf1afcfd6" for image with ID "9fa75b159e9a9f4678c27b9f79822c657e54623873c1610da58d971fa0adbe86" (consider removing the image to resolve the issue): file does not exist
```

### Describe the results you expected

The `podman images` command should never ever break.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Fedora 37 laptop with latest (current) `podman-4.6.1-1.fc37.x86_64` RPM installed.

### Additional information

Ran a `podman system reset` twice before reproducing twice.  The `podman images` command consistently returns an error and exits 125.

comments.body: I'll take a look at this.
FWIW I hit the same issue on F38 (podman 4.6.2)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This is still an issue on F40 (podman-5.0.3-1.fc40.x86_64)
